'''Duncan MacGillivray''' (born 25 October 1976) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|pr}} in the 1990s and 2000s. He played at club level for the [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]] in the [[NRL]], and the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] in the [[Super League]], and at international level for [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]].

==Background==
He was born in [[Darwin, Northern Territory]], Australia.

MacGillivray is a [[Nightcliff Dragons]] junior playing in the [[Northern Territory Rugby League|Darwin Rugby League]] competition.

==Playing career==
He went on to making his name in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] spending five years with the [[Penrith Panthers]], and playing for [[South Sydney Rabbitohs]].

The former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international had an excellent season with the Wildcats in 2006 and his form carried on into the following years. He retired at the end of the 2008 season after 5-seasons of service to the Wildcats.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.ssralmanac.com/mobile/players_m/player_macgillivray_duncan.htm|title=South Sydney Rabbitohs Rugby League Player Report - Duncan MacGillivray|website=ssralmanac.com}}</ref>
